title: Publisher Confirms, Windows, Failure Ambiguity, and Republish · Inference Brief: Publisher Ledger Cleanup
summary: A producer opens a normal channel, calls waitForConfirms after every message, discards sequence identity, and blindly republishes all work after a connection loss even when the broker may already own it. Planned artifact: Add and verify the publisher ledger cleanup increment in confirm-window and ambiguous-publication ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.
Classify unresolved confirms after failure as ambiguous, retain stable message identity, and republish only through a duplicate-safe downstream contract.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Publisher Ledger Cleanup · Theory Publisher Ledger Cleanup
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 4312 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound the confirm window and preserve ambiguity when a channel closes before proof; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Retire confirmed entries, reject nacked entries, preserve ambiguous entries, cancel waiters, and prove zero memory or callback leaks across channel generations.
